title: Fix: Stop Loss column, Alpha Vantage price display, and admin field visibility toggles

requirements.text: In TradeListPage.tsx, remove the 'Result' column from the trade table header and all trade rows, and add a 'Stop Loss' column in its place. The stopLoss field value must be read from each trade object and formatted as a price. The Result field must remain in the Trade type, backend data model, and Trade Detail page — only the Trade List table column is changed. Preserve all existing column styling, responsive mobile card layout, and sort/filter behavior.

quotes: Replace 'Result' with 'Stop Loss' in the main trade table.

acceptanceCriteria: The Trade List table header does NOT contain a 'Result' column
The Trade List table header DOES contain a 'Stop Loss' column
Each trade row displays the stopLoss value in the Stop Loss column
The Trade Detail page still shows the Result field
Mobile card layout reflects the same column change
All existing filters, sorting, and styling remain intact

requirements.text: Create or fully rewrite frontend/src/hooks/useMarketPrice.ts. The hook accepts a ticker symbol string and fetches delayed market price data from the Alpha Vantage GLOBAL_QUOTE endpoint (https://www.alphavantage.co/query?function=GLOBAL_QUOTE&symbol=SYMBOL&apikey=KEY). The API key must be read from the Vite environment variable VITE_ALPHA_VANTAGE_API_KEY defined in a .env file at the project root. Cache each ticker's response in localStorage with a timestamp; skip re-fetching if the cached entry is less than 60 minutes old. The hook must expose: currentPrice (number | null), lastUpdated (string | null), and isLoading (boolean). On API error or missing key, currentPrice and lastUpdated must be null and no error must be thrown to the caller.

quotes: Integrate Alpha Vantage API and display delayed current price.

acceptanceCriteria: useMarketPrice hook file exists at frontend/src/hooks/useMarketPrice.ts
Hook reads API key from import.meta.env.VITE_ALPHA_VANTAGE_API_KEY
Hook calls Alpha Vantage GLOBAL_QUOTE endpoint with the provided symbol
Responses are cached in localStorage per ticker for at least 60 minutes
Hook returns { currentPrice, lastUpdated, isLoading }
If API key is missing or request fails, currentPrice and lastUpdated are null without throwing

requirements.text: In TradeListPage.tsx, integrate the useMarketPrice hook (REQ-23) for each trade row. Display 'Current Price (Delayed): [value]' and 'Last Updated: [time]' within the trade row or beneath the script name. Add a single disclaimer line below the table: 'Market data is delayed and for reference only.' Style the price display using the existing gold/amber accent tokens. Show a subtle loading indicator while isLoading is true. If currentPrice is null, show a dash or 'N/A'.

quotes: Integrate Alpha Vantage API and display delayed current price.

acceptanceCriteria: Each trade row in the Trade List displays 'Current Price (Delayed):' with the fetched value or 'N/A'
Each trade row shows 'Last Updated:' with the timestamp or 'N/A'
A disclaimer 'Market data is delayed and for reference only.' appears once below the table
A loading indicator is shown while the price is being fetched
Styling uses existing gold/amber accent colors and matches the app's finance theme

requirements.text: In TradeDetailPage.tsx, integrate the useMarketPrice hook (REQ-23) using the trade's scriptName as the ticker symbol. Inside the Pricing section card, display 'Current Price (Delayed): [value]' and 'Last Updated: [time]'. Add the disclaimer 'Market data is delayed and for reference only.' within or directly below the Pricing section. If currentPrice is null, display 'N/A'. Show a loading state while fetching.

quotes: Integrate Alpha Vantage API and display delayed current price.

acceptanceCriteria: Trade Detail Pricing section shows 'Current Price (Delayed):' with fetched value or 'N/A'
Trade Detail Pricing section shows 'Last Updated:' with timestamp or 'N/A'
Disclaimer 'Market data is delayed and for reference only.' is present in or below the Pricing section
Loading state is shown while price data is being fetched

requirements.text: Fully rewrite the Field Visibility Settings panel in AdminPage.tsx to fix non-functional toggle controls. The rewrite must: (1) on component mount, call getFieldVisibilityConfig via the backend actor and populate local React state with the returned map of fieldName -> visibility level; (2) render each field as a labeled toggle switch whose checked state is driven by the local React state; (3) when a toggle is changed, immediately call setFieldVisibility(fieldName, newLevel) on the backend actor and update local state upon successful response; (4) on page refresh, re-fetch getFieldVisibilityConfig so persisted values are restored; (5) display a visual loading/saving indicator during async calls; (6) handle errors gracefully with a user-facing message. Do not change the layout of the panel or affect the User Role Management section or the trade CRUD section.

quotes: Fix admin field visibility toggles so they save and apply.
Field visibility toggles still do not work

acceptanceCriteria: On load, toggles reflect the current state fetched from getFieldVisibilityConfig
Clicking a toggle calls setFieldVisibility on the backend actor with the correct fieldName and new level
After the call completes, the toggle visually reflects the new state
On page refresh, toggled values are correctly restored from the backend
A loading/saving indicator is shown during async operations
Errors surface as a user-facing message without crashing the page
The User Role Management section and trade CRUD section are unaffected

requirements.text: Add a VITE_ALPHA_VANTAGE_API_KEY entry to the project's .env file (and .env.example if it exists) so the Alpha Vantage API key is available to the Vite build. The value in .env should be set to 'demo' as a safe default so the app builds and runs without requiring a real key. Document in a comment that developers must replace 'demo' with a real key for production use.

quotes: Integrate Alpha Vantage API and display delayed current price.

acceptanceCriteria: .env file contains VITE_ALPHA_VANTAGE_API_KEY=demo
A comment in .env explains that 'demo' should be replaced with a real Alpha Vantage API key
The Vite build does not fail due to missing environment variable

constraints: Do NOT rebuild the project or reset any existing data
Do NOT modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths
The Result field must remain in the Trade type, Motoko backend, and Trade Detail page — only the Trade List table column visibility changes
The User Role Management section and trade CRUD section in AdminPage.tsx must not be altered
Alpha Vantage integration must be frontend-only; no backend proxy or caching in Motoko
Do not change the Trade List or Trade Detail page layout beyond the specified column and price additions
nonGoals: Rebuilding or resetting the project from scratch
Changing the backend data model for trades
Adding new trade fields beyond what already exists
Modifying authentication or Internet Identity logic
Changing the User Role Management panel behavior
